command_surface.surface_model: TigerKit v7.2 exposes Claude Code plugin commands; commands/*.md and .claude-plugin/plugin.json own /tk:* contracts without separate repo-local skill files.

evals.name: command-surface-restores-existing-commands
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit active command surface. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:spec, /tk:gap, /tk:reflect, /tk:handoff, and /tk:meta-feedback as active user-facing commands. Should explain that TigerKit v7.2 exposes Claude Code plugin commands, not separate repo-local skill files, and that commands/*.md plus .claude-plugin/plugin.json own the /tk:* contracts. Should explain that handoff and meta-feedback are active existing commands, not newly invented commands. Should explain that spec/gap write branch-local working memory, reflect creates durable insight, handoff creates continuation context, and meta-feedback extracts generalized TigerKit improvement feedback from session history.
expectations: Treats /tk:* as command-owned plugin contracts, not separate repo-local skills
Treats handoff as active continuation command
Treats meta-feedback as active session-history improvement command

evals.name: gap-default-user-facing-surface
evals.prompt: Evaluate the default /tk:gap user-facing command surface. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should describe default /tk:gap as a single user-facing gap review execution that compares Product/Design Spec, implementation plan, and current implementation. Should say default artifacts are report.md and run.json. Should say default stdout is compact and action-oriented with run ID, branch scope, P0/P1/P2 counts, source conflict count, clarification-needed count, report path, run.json path, and next action. Should not require input-manifest.json, contracts.json, candidates.json, judge-result.json, baseline-snapshot.json, heuristicProof, performance, baseline scores, or proof artifact inventory in the default path. Should preserve existing /tk:gap command and not create a new skill or remove an existing skill.
expectations: Default artifacts are report.md and run.json
Default stdout is compact and action-oriented

evals.name: gap-maintainer-proof-is-explicit-only
evals.expected_output: Should describe --maintainer-proof as maintainer/self-eval only, not as a user-facing quality mode or recommended stronger preset. Should allow proof/debug artifacts only when --maintainer-proof is explicit, under maintainer-proof/. Should allow input-manifest.json, contracts.json, candidates.json, judge-result.json, baseline-snapshot.json, proof.json, heuristicProof, performance, baselineAutoRefreshGate, claimFreshnessGate, targetSurfaceCoverageGate, and dispatchCompletenessGate only in the maintainer-proof path. Should still keep default stdout compact even when proof artifacts exist unless --print-report is used.
expectations: Does not describe maintainer proof as user quality mode
Allows heuristicProof only in maintainer path
Allows performance proof only in maintainer path

evals.name: gap-analysis-depth-user-facing-boundary
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap analysis depth selection. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should choose direct only for one explicit local surface with no API/DTO/state/auth/payment/data mutation/shared component/ambiguity trigger. Should choose bounded for single screen/component/command with nearby 1-depth usage lookup or representative samples up to 3. Should choose expanded for shared component, design-system, API/DTO/state transition, source conflict risk, inaccessible reference, ambiguous Product/API/Design decision, or divergent similar implementations. Should choose exhaustive-capped for P0/P1 candidate, auth/permission/payment/data mutation/destructive action, release gate, or cross-module impact. Hard triggers should run before riskScore tie-breakers. Explicit --analysis-depth should not lower the heuristic-required minimum depth for risky surfaces. Should not call the choice lite or strict mode. Should not require analysisDepth proof, riskScore, depthReasons dump, baseline, or improvement ratio in default stdout/report/run.json; those are maintainer-proof only if claimed.
expectations: Hard triggers run before riskScore tie-breakers

evals.name: gap-consumer-fallback-not-producer-absence
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap candidate that claims an API does not provide a required value, but cites only frontend fallback/default/mapper evidence. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not accept the candidate as P0/P1/P2 and should not create SourceConflict from consumer-only evidence. If API contract, schema, endpoint response, serializer, data model, backend fixture, or owner-confirmed producer behavior is provided or discoverable in the repo, it should inspect that producer surface before asking the user. Should reject or downgrade with missing_producer_evidence, missing_evidence, or unverifiable only after available producer surfaces are checked. Should state producer-side evidence is required, tell the user what producer evidence was checked and what is still missing in report.md, and create a Clarification Needed confirmation request when the missing producer evidence blocks the gap decision.
expectations: Checks provided or discoverable producer evidence before asking user
Uses missing_producer_evidence only after available producer surfaces are checked
Does not accept SourceConflict without producer evidence
Creates Clarification Needed for missing producer evidence when decision-blocking

evals.name: gap-ambiguity-blocks-judge-accept-path
evals.prompt: Evaluate a /tk:gap candidate where Product/API/Design source priority is ambiguous. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not silently choose the likely interpretation. Should route the item to SourceConflict or Clarification Needed before accepted finding path. Should continue other clear checks. Should record evidence, impact, recommendation, and status for the clarification, using user-facing short Ref such as Q1.
expectations: Uses short Ref for user-facing clarification

evals.name: gap-final-receipt-single-execution
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap stdout receipt for a completed default run. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should output a compact user receipt with run ID, branch scope, P0/P1/P2 counts, source conflict count, clarification-needed count, report path, run.json path, and one next action. Should include compact Actionable Findings only when accepted findings exist, and compact Clarification Needed only when user confirmation is needed. Should use short run-local Ref values instead of long canonical Candidate/Finding IDs. Should not print analysis depth, expansion reasons, verification escalation, dispatch skip summary, Candidate Intake Gate summary, TargetSurfaceCoverageGate proof, DispatchCompletenessGate proof, BaselineAutoRefreshGate status, ClaimFreshnessGate status, heuristic proof ratios, rejected/downgraded observation lists, numeric performance proof, or artifact file lists in default stdout. Should not output executed preset, recommended preset, or Strict executed as primary labels.
expectations: Includes report path, run.json path, and next action
Keeps proof and baseline details out of default stdout
Keeps rejected/downgraded observation list out of default stdout
Keeps artifact file list out of default stdout
Compact tables use short Ref values instead of long canonical IDs

evals.name: gap-default-excludes-proof-metadata
evals.prompt: Evaluate whether default /tk:gap should create or require proof metadata. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should say default /tk:gap does not require heuristicProof, performance, baseline-snapshot.json, input-manifest.json, judge-result.json, qualityGates dump, dispatchPlan dump, dispatchSkips proof, targetSurfaceCoverageGate proof, dispatchCompletenessGate proof, claimFreshnessGate, baselineAutoRefreshGate, false-positive improvement claim, false-negative improvement claim, speed improvement claim, analysis-depth improvement claim, or combined heuristic proof claim. Should say those are allowed only with explicit --maintainer-proof. Should still preserve evidence read-back, producer evidence gate, ambiguity gate, and JudgeMergerAgent decision quality in the default path.
expectations: No default input-manifest or judge-result requirement

evals.name: gap-maintainer-performance-proof
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap --maintainer-proof performance proof requirements. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require a measured performance proof rather than a vague estimate only when --maintainer-proof is explicit. Should use the contract-derived critical path proxy when runtime wall-clock instrumentation is unavailable. Should require speed proof to record cumulativeBaseline, refreshed origin/main iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, score direction lower_is_better, performance agent critical path groups, deterministic stage groups, run procedure steps, measurementMethod, baselineAutoRefreshGate, baseline-snapshot.json, and heuristicProof.speed in maintainer-proof artifacts. Should calculate baseline and current with the same proxy weights. Credited dispatchSkips may count only when credited, criticalPathDelta, and evidenceCoveragePreserved are recorded. Should not claim success from phrases such as expected, estimated, or likely.
expectations: Requires measurementMethod and critical path components
Requires cumulative and refreshed origin/main iteration speed baselines
Requires credited dispatch skip fields before counting skip benefit

evals.name: gap-maintainer-false-negative-coverage-proof
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap --maintainer-proof for a run that could miss a P0/P1 issue because target surfaces or skipped agents were not checked. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require TargetSurfaceCoverageGate, DispatchCompletenessGate, and ClaimFreshnessGate before claiming false-negative improvement only in maintainer-proof mode. TargetSurfaceCoverageGate should record checked contract surfaces, target/plan/producer surfaces, and unchecked required surfaces for the selected analysisDepth. DispatchCompletenessGate should compare SourcePresenceManifest with dispatchPlan and record falseNegativeRisk for skipped agents. CriticalRedTeamAgent should perform one missed-P0/P1 targeted search within the selected depth scope. heuristicProof.falseNegative should include metric, denominator, cumulativeBaseline, iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, improvement formula, and claimAllowed, and claimAllowed should be false if required surfaces are unchecked, dispatch completeness is missing, or proof inputs are stale.
expectations: Records falseNegativeRisk for skipped agents in maintainer proof
Runs missed P0/P1 targeted search once
Blocks false-negative claim when required coverage is missing

evals.name: gap-maintainer-combined-heuristic-proof-claim
evals.prompt: Evaluate whether /tk:gap --maintainer-proof may claim cumulative and per-iteration improvement for false-positive reduction, false-negative reduction, speed, and analysis depth. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should require explicit --maintainer-proof before any combined improvement claim. Should require heuristicProof cumulativeRequiredImprovementRatio and iterationRequiredImprovementRatio. For falsePositive, falseNegative, speed, and analysisDepth, should require metric, scoreDirection, cumulativeBaseline, iterationBaseline, currentScore, cumulativeImprovementRatio, iterationImprovementRatio, improvementFormula, and claimAllowed. Higher-is-better metrics should use currentScore / baselineScore; speed should use baselineScore / currentScore. Should require ClaimFreshnessGate status, checkedAfter, staleInputs, and repairedInputs as a separate claim gate rather than a score denominator. Should allow the combined claim only when all four cumulative ratios meet the cumulative target, all four iteration ratios are > 1.0, all four claimAllowed values are true, and ClaimFreshnessGate passes. Should reject the combined claim if fixed cumulative baseline alone is used for a new iteration claim, consumer-only producer evidence, unchecked target surfaces, missing dispatch completeness, uncredited dispatch skips, risk-score-before-hard-trigger depth selection, or stale proof inputs are used.
expectations: Requires ClaimFreshnessGate as separate claim gate
Rejects combined claim if any cumulative or iteration subproof is below threshold
Rejects fixed cumulative baseline used as iteration proof

evals.name: handoff-remains-continuation-command
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:handoff after a branch has spec and gap artifacts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should preserve /tk:handoff as the existing continuation command. It should write the canonical branch-local handoff current.md by default, record latestHandoffPath in .claude/tigerkit/global-index.json for pathless resume instructions, include Reader Guide and Resume Prompt, optionally mention latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths when observed, and create a dated archive only when archive=true or an explicit archive request is present.
expectations: Writes branch-local handoff current.md by default
Records latestHandoffPath in global-index.json for pathless resume
Can reference latest branch-local Spec Patch and Gap Run paths
Does not create dated archive without archive=true or explicit archive request

evals.name: meta-feedback-uses-session-history-for-gap-improvements
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:meta-feedback after a session where the user said gap was slow, backend findings had false positives, mode wording was confusing, and ambiguous UI/API decisions should be resolved with the user. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should use the session history as evidence, generalize the feedback without exposing project assets, and propose TigerKit command or skill improvements. It should capture performance friction as a maintainer/self-eval concern only when relevant, candidate evidence precision gates for stale file:line/module-path coordinates, producer-absence evidence gates for backend false-positive reduction, ambiguity consent gate guidance, and UI clarification via tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups. It should not create repo rule patches like reflect and should not output raw user quotes, paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels.
expectations: Uses session history as the evidence source
Generalizes performance friction without making default /tk:gap proof-heavy
Generalizes candidate evidence precision gate improvement
Mentions tables or aligned TUI/ASCII mockups for UI clarification
Proposes TigerKit command or skill improvement
Does not expose raw user quotes
Does not expose paths, tickets, PR numbers, branch names, or project-specific labels
Does not behave like /tk:reflect repo rule patching

evals.name: reflect-applies-directly-to-rules-and-claude
evals.prompt: You are evaluating /tk:reflect after branch-local gap runs produced durable repo-wide insight candidates. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should default to apply=true, read branch-local TigerKit memory, write directly to CLAUDE.local.md, CLAUDE.md, or .claude/rules/* depending on target scope, prefer CLAUDE.local.md for CLAUDE.md-targeted insights when CLAUDE.local.md exists, record even project-wide guidance in CLAUDE.local.md first and suggest CLAUDE.md promotion instead of silently writing shared instructions to CLAUDE.md, treat --dry-run and --apply=false as preview-only aliases, submit /tk:meta-feedback proposal-only after reflect processing by default, allow --no-meta-feedback or --meta-feedback=false to opt out, avoid modifying source code, dedupe repeated insights, create or update the current branch entry in global-index.json for apply bookkeeping, not promote session observation patterns when specs/gap artifacts are absent unless the user explicitly confirmed a TigerKit operating rule, and avoid creating a separate tigerkit-reflections sidecar file.
expectations: Does not promote session observation patterns when specs/gap artifacts are absent unless explicitly user-confirmed
Creates or updates current branch entry in global-index.json for apply bookkeeping
Writes directly to CLAUDE.local.md, CLAUDE.md, or .claude/rules/*
Prefers CLAUDE.local.md for CLAUDE.md-targeted insights when CLAUDE.local.md exists
Suggests CLAUDE.md promotion instead of writing shared guidance there when CLAUDE.local.md is managed
Does not create tigerkit-reflections sidecar file

evals.name: gap-source-by-source-intake-and-design-trust-axis
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap behavior when the user provides many design sources across breakpoints, variants, and auxiliary cases, including both structural metadata and visual captures. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend source-by-source locked intake when a large source set would blur independent verification: first state the preferred delivery format, then collect, measure, compare, and lock each source before proceeding. It should preserve small-source batch intake when independent verification is not at risk. It should classify design evidence into structural_context and visual_capture, use visual_capture only for layout/composition/state/hierarchy support, and avoid confirming color, spacing, dimensions, typography scale, or other numeric design values from visual_capture alone. Numeric design values should require structural metadata, design tokens, or explicit source/user confirmation, with existing implementation used only as comparison or inference support rather than design SOT; otherwise the result should become Clarification Needed or rejected unverifiable rather than an accepted finding.
expectations: Recommends source-by-source locked intake for large source sets
States preferred delivery format before large intake
Locks each source after measurement and comparison
Preserves batch intake for small or inherently bundled source sets
Uses visual_capture for layout and composition only
Does not confirm numeric design values from visual_capture alone
Requires structural metadata, design tokens, or explicit confirmation for numeric design values
Creates Clarification Needed or rejected unverifiable when numeric evidence is insufficient

evals.name: reflect-source-authority-backlog-and-meta-boundaries
evals.prompt: Evaluate TigerKit docs after reviewing dryforge and oh-my-opencode-slim reflect ideas. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should say source material is not authority and only confirmed or explicitly scoped items may become gap evidence or durable guidance. Should describe /tk:reflect as a durable repo guidance promotion gate that inventories existing guidance first, scores candidates with Frequency, Cost, Risk, Stability, and Coverage, supports Needs more evidence, and treats promoting nothing as a successful outcome. Should describe /tk:handoff as the place for a branch-local Pending Backlog with source, evidence, priority, blocked-by, and suggested next action rather than introducing separate active backlog/drain commands. Should describe /tk:gap as optionally including a graph-lite Next Action Graph for clarification/fix/re-check ordering while staying out of full runner or execution graph ownership. Should keep /tk:meta-feedback focused on TigerKit command or skill improvements and keep repo durable guidance in /tk:reflect, with agent runtime/config, MCP permission, and custom agent recommendations outside TigerKit core scope.
expectations: Requires confirmed or explicitly scoped items for gap evidence or durable guidance
Scores reflect candidates with Frequency, Cost, Risk, Stability, and Coverage
Inventories existing durable guidance before reflect apply
Supports Needs more evidence for reflect
Treats no durable insight promoted as successful reflect outcome
Uses handoff Pending Backlog for follow-up queue
Does not introduce separate active backlog or drain commands
Adds graph-lite Next Action Graph to gap report
Does not make TigerKit a full runner or execution graph owner
Keeps agent runtime/config, MCP permission, and custom agent recommendations outside TigerKit core scope
